zh:
约鲁巴神话是西非几内亚湾约鲁巴人的信仰世界，今日在尼日利亚、贝宁与多哥仍由数千万人口口相传，又随移民散播到大西洋两岸，是传播最广的活态传统宗教之一。它没有写定的古代经典，全部知识都装在人的记忆里：伊法占卜的256个卦象各系一库诗文，颂神的"奥里基"赞诗、节庆、鼓乐与假面舞承担着经书的职能。19世纪末以来，塞缪尔·约翰逊的《约鲁巴人史》与伊多武、巴斯科姆、阿宾博拉等学者的记录，才把这个口传世界陆续写成文字。

体系的顶端是遥远的至高神奥罗杜马雷。他不设神庙，把世界交给数百位"奥里沙"打理——相传共有四百零一位。奥里沙各有司职、圣色、禁忌与鼓点：奥巴塔拉塑人，奥贡开路，尚戈掌雷，奥孙、叶莫嘉、奥雅各主一条大河；信使埃舒守着十字路口，把祭品与讯息在天地间传递。人与神的关系十分务实：献祭换庇护，占卜问前程，命运是出生前自己跪选的，又可凭"阿谢"——成事之力——去经营。圣城伊费被奉为大地开辟之处，诸王室都把血脉追到造地之神奥杜杜瓦名下。

大西洋奴隶贸易把这套神话整体搬过了海：古巴的桑特里亚、巴西的坎东布莱、特立尼达的奥里沙教都以奥里沙崇拜为骨架，里约的新年夜有数百万人向海浪抛掷白花献给叶莫嘉。2005年起，伊法占卜体系与奥孙-奥绍博圣林先后进入联合国教科文组织的人类遗产名录；图图奥拉、索因卡、奥克瑞的小说与诗歌，则把棕榈酒鬼、奥贡与阿比库灵童写进了世界文学。
en:
Yoruba mythology is the living belief-world of the Yoruba people of the Gulf of Guinea, carried today by tens of millions in Nigeria, Benin, and Togo, and by one of the largest religious diasporas on earth. It has no ancient written canon; its scripture is memory. The 256 odu of Ifa divination each head a corpus of verses, while oriki praise poetry, festivals, drumming, and masquerade do the work of books. Only from the late nineteenth century did Samuel Johnson's History of the Yorubas and the studies of Idowu, Bascom, and Abimbola begin fixing the oral world in print.

At the summit stands Olodumare, the remote high god without temples, who leaves the world to the orishas — 401 of them, tradition says. Each has a portfolio, colors, taboos, and drum rhythms: Obatala shapes human bodies, Ogun clears the road, Shango wields thunder, Oshun, Yemoja, and Oya each command a river, and the trickster Eshu keeps the crossroads, ferrying offerings and messages between heaven and earth. Relations between people and gods are practical: sacrifice buys protection, divination settles a course, and destiny, chosen on one's knees before birth, is worked out with ase, the power that makes things happen. The holy city of Ile-Ife is where the land began, and every royal line runs back to Oduduwa, who made it.

The Atlantic slave trade carried the whole system across the ocean: Cuban Santeria, Brazilian Candomble, and Trinidad's Orisha religion are built on orisha worship, and on New Year's Eve millions in Rio cast white flowers into the sea for Yemoja. Since 2005 the Ifa divination system and the Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove have entered UNESCO's heritage lists, while Tutuola, Soyinka, and Okri wrote the palm-wine ghosts, Ogun, and the abiku spirit child into world literature.
